opinion.h: MAYBERRY v. PENNSYLVANIA. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE SUPREME COURT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 11, Misc. Decided December 13, 1965.
opinion.p: The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for a writ of certiorari are granted. Upon consideration of the concessions of the State and an examination of the papers in the case, the judgment of the Supreme Court of Pennsylvania is vacated and the case remanded to that court for further proceedings.
382 U.S. 286 O'CONNOR v. OHIO. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OHIO. No. 281, Misc. Decided December 13, 1965.
Rehearing granted: certiorari granted: vacated and remanded.

The petition for rehearing is granted and the order of October 11, 1965, insofar as it denies certiorari, is vacated. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is granted and the judgment is vacated. The case is remanded to the Supreme Court of Ohio for further proceedings in light of Griffin v. California, 380 U.S. 609.
